title: Cabbage Roll Soup
description: A cozy, one-pot meal that delivers all the rich, savory flavors of traditional European cabbage rolls without the fuss of rolling or baking. This hearty soup combines ground beef, sausage, tender cabbage, and rice in a tangy tomato broth that will warm you from the inside out - the ultimate comfort food for any season.
ingredients: 1/2 lb ground sausage (pork sausage such as Italian or breakfast-style)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2 medium carrots, quartered lengthwise and sliced
5 cups chopped cabbage (packed, about 1/2 medium head)
1 (14.5 oz) can petite diced tomatoes (with juices)
3/4 cup dry jasmine or long-grain white rice
Juice from 1/2 of a small lemon (about 1 Tablespoon)
instructions: Heat a Dutch oven or large soup pot over medium-high heat. Add 1 lb ground beef and 1/2 lb ground sausage. Season lightly with salt and pepper. Cook, breaking the meat into small pieces with a wooden spoon, until well browned, about 6-8 minutes. Drain excess fat if needed.

Add 1 chopped large yellow onion and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2 minutes until slightly softened. Add 4 minced garlic cloves and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant (don't let it burn). Add 2 quartered and sliced carrots and 5 cups chopped cabbage and cook for 2 minutes, stirring to combine.

Add 5 cups beef broth, 2 cans tomato sauce, 1 can diced tomatoes with their juices, 1 Tablespoon Worcestershire sauce, 1 1/2 teaspoons smoked paprika, 1 teaspoon oregano, 1/2 teaspoon thyme, and 1 bay leaf. Stir well and bring to a boil. Stir in 3/4 cup dry rice.

Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 25-30 minutes, stirring once or twice during cooking, until the rice is tender and the cabbage is soft. If the soup looks too thick, stir in the remaining 1 cup of beef broth or more to your preference.
Remove bay leaf. Stir in lemon juice (about 1 Tablespoon) and 2 Tablespoons fresh parsley. Taste and season with additional salt and pepper as needed. Ladle into bowls and serve hot.
notes: For gluten-free: use gluten-free Worcestershire sauce and ensure beef broth is gluten-free certified.
Rice will continue to absorb broth as the soup sits and especially when stored overnight. When reheating leftovers, add a splash of broth or water to loosen it up.
Don't substitute instant/minute rice - it will disintegrate. Use standard dry jasmine or long-grain white rice.
Chop cabbage into roughly 1-inch pieces for the best spoon-friendly texture.
Optional: top with a few dashes of crushed red pepper flakes for a little kick.
